description: Purple-team validation scenarios mapping each detection to a safe, simulation-based adversary behaviour. Every scenario is exercised against synthetic telemetry generated by the labs - none of this provides instructions to compromise real systems.
safety_note: This validation pack uses synthetic telemetry only and does not provide instructions to compromise real systems.

scenarios.objective: Wear a user down with repeated MFA prompts until one is approved
scenarios.expected_telemetry: 5+ SigninLogs events with ResultType 500121 for one user within 10 minutes, followed by an approved MFA sign-in within 30 minutes
scenarios.expected_detection: High alert (Critical when the follow-on approval is present)
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: Did the user actually approve, and from what device and location?
scenarios.expected_containment: Revoke sessions (auto at Critical), reset credential, re-register MFA
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: A single missed prompt during device re-enrolment with no burst
scenarios.validation_method: Generator emits the burst + approval; assert exactly one Critical alert and that a lone missed prompt does not fire

scenarios.objective: Use stolen credentials from a location the real user cannot have travelled to
scenarios.expected_telemetry: Two successful sign-ins for one user implying travel faster than 900 km/h
scenarios.expected_detection: Medium alert, escalated by privilege/off-hours/risk modifiers
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: Is the second IP corporate VPN egress, or genuinely foreign infrastructure?
scenarios.expected_containment: Attribute the IP; revoke sessions if unexplained
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: Both endpoints inside the corporate VPN egress range 198.51.100.0/24
scenarios.validation_method: Tuned run (v1.1.0) suppresses the VPN pair while keeping the true positive; asserted in the test suite

scenarios.objective: Weaken authentication controls after gaining an admin foothold
scenarios.expected_telemetry: AuditLogs 'Update conditional access policy' event disabling an MFA-for-admins policy
scenarios.expected_detection: High alert, escalated when the actor holds no CA management role
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: Is there an approved change record, and does the actor own CA policy?
scenarios.expected_containment: Revert the policy; treat the actor as compromised
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: Approved change-window modification by the identity platform team
scenarios.validation_method: Synthetic audit event with a non-CA-admin actor; assert High-to-Critical alert

scenarios.objective: Plant durable persistence that survives user password resets and MFA
scenarios.expected_telemetry: AuditLogs 'Add service principal credentials' event against a high-privilege app
scenarios.expected_detection: Critical alert only when the target SP is high-privilege
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: Was this an approved rotation, and what can this SP reach?
scenarios.expected_containment: Remove the added credential (auto-safe); rotate remaining secrets (approval)
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: Approved secret rotation on a low-privilege monitoring principal
scenarios.validation_method: Two synthetic events (high-priv and low-priv SP); assert only the high-priv one fires

scenarios.objective: Escalate to a protected directory role without going through approval
scenarios.expected_telemetry: AuditLogs role-activation event for Global Administrator / Application Administrator with no linked ticket
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: Is there an approved PIM request behind this activation?
scenarios.expected_containment: Deactivate the role; review actions taken while elevated (approval-gated)
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: Break-glass activation with a linked change ticket
scenarios.validation_method: Synthetic ticketless activation fires; a ticketed one does not (asserted)

scenarios.objective: Open an internal management surface to the internet
scenarios.expected_telemetry: AzureActivity NSG securityRules/write creating an Allow rule with source 0.0.0.0/0
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: Was this an approved change, and what became reachable?
scenarios.expected_containment: Revert the NSG rule (approval + snapshot for rollback)
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: An internal-only rule (source 10.0.0.0/8) or a deliberate tagged public endpoint
scenarios.validation_method: Synthetic public rule fires; the internal 10.0.0.0/8 rule does not (asserted)

scenarios.objective: Make a management port reachable so a login attempt becomes possible
scenarios.expected_telemetry: Internet-open NSG rule on port 3389/22 protecting a VM that has a public IP
scenarios.expected_detection: Critical alert (reachability confirmed by the public IP)
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: Is the endpoint actually reachable, and are there inbound attempts?
scenarios.expected_containment: Restrict via JIT/Bastion or isolate the VM (approval-gated)
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: An open management rule on a VM with no public IP (permitted but not reachable)
scenarios.validation_method: Public IP present in asset inventory drives the alert; absence suppresses it

scenarios.objective: Harvest privileged credentials from a Tier-0 vault outside change control
scenarios.expected_telemetry: 4+ ticketless checkouts within an hour, or any ticketless checkout 00:00-05:00 local, from the domain-admins safe
scenarios.expected_detection: Medium alert, escalated for the Tier-0 safe
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: Is there an emergency-change record, and do PSM recordings match?
scenarios.expected_containment: Force check-in, rotate credentials, review session recordings (approval)
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: Genuine emergency change with a retrospectively raised ticket
scenarios.validation_method: Synthetic quiet-hours ticketless burst fires; ticketed daytime checkouts do not

scenarios.objective: Identify dormant privileged identities an attacker could quietly reuse
scenarios.expected_telemetry: Identity inventory: enabled privileged accounts with no sign-in for 60+ days or none ever
scenarios.expected_detection: Low posture alert; Medium/High for orphaned service accounts
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: Is this account still needed, and who owns it?
scenarios.expected_containment: Disable or convert to PIM-eligible with expiry (change-managed)
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: An intentionally dormant break-glass account excluded by naming convention
scenarios.validation_method: Inventory fixtures include three stale accounts; assert all three surface

scenarios.objective: Walk from a phished login to an internet-exposed management endpoint
scenarios.expected_telemetry: Seven correlated stages across sign-in, audit, activity and NSG telemetry sharing one identity, service principal and resource group
scenarios.expected_detection: One Critical correlated incident with a blast-radius score of 100/100
scenarios.expected_analyst_question: What is the blast radius, and which single control would have broken the chain earliest?
scenarios.expected_containment: Enrich, then approval-gated containment across the whole chain, then RCA
scenarios.false_positive_scenario: Three unrelated benign events on a shared service account within the window (mitigated by entity scoping)
scenarios.validation_method: Full chain generated; assert exactly one Critical chain incident spanning stages 1-7
